zh.lifeSummary: 當William Davis於大約1591年出生在英國武斯特斯爾阿爾時，他的父親David Davis52歲，母親Angharrad Jevan42歲。他於1621年11月30日與Martha Wakeman在英國武斯特斯爾貝德利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1659年10月21日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Davis于大约1591年出生在英国武斯特斯爾阿爾时，他的父亲David Davis52岁，母亲Angharrad Jevan42岁。他于1621年11月30日与Martha Wakeman在英国武斯特斯爾貝德利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1659年10月21日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂。

nameMeanings.name: Davis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: patronymic meaning ‘Dafydd's (son)’, equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd, the Welsh form of David . The spelling Davis is more typical in southwestern England northwards as far as Lancashire, where the frequency of the surname largely reflects Welsh migration, but may sometimes represent a native English surname based on Davy (compare Davies ). Davis (including in the sense 2 below) is the eighth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans.
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Daibhéid ‘son of David’; see McDevitt . Compare Davies .

History: John Davis or Davys (c. 1550–1605) was an English navigator who searched for the Northwest Passage. — By the 18th century there were numerous persons named Davis in America, including the jurist John Davis, born in 1761 in Plymouth, MA, and Henry Davis, a clergyman and college president, who was born in 1771 in East Hampton, NY. — Jefferson Davis, born in 1808 in KY, was president of the Confederate States of America from 1861 to 1865.

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Davies
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of Davis , a patronymic equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd ‘Dafydd's son’. The name is especially numerous in south Wales. This form of the name probably shows the influence of Davy , the common English form of the personal name David in the late medieval and early modern periods (see Davey ).

variants.name: Daves
meanings: Welsh and English: probably a variant of   Davis   or   Davies  .
In some cases also German (Däves): from a shortened form of the personal name Matthäus (see Matthew ). This surname is very rare in Germany.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of German   Tewes  , a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
On April 24, 1638, a company of five-hundred English Puritans led by the Reverend John Davenport and Theophilus Eaton, a wealthy London merchant, sailed into the harbor.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When William Davis was born about 1591, in Arley, Worcestershire, England, his father, David Davis, was 52 and his mother, Angharrad Jevan, was 42. He married Martha Wakeman on 30 November 1621, in Bewdley, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died before 21 October 1659, in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: William Davisが約1591年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアアルリーで生まれた時，父David Davisは52歳，母Angharrad Jevanは42歳であった。1621年11月30日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベウドリーでMartha Wakemanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1659年10月21日以前にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニューヘイベン トウンで死去した。

ko.New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, Briti: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운
ko.Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Britis: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 보스턴
ko.Roxbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Briti: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 록스버이
ko.New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐
ko.lifeSummary: William Davis(이)가 대략 1591년 영국 워스스터실르 알리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David Davis(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Angharrad Jevan(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1621년 11월 30일 영국 워스스터실르 브w들리에서 Martha Wakeman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1659년 10월 21일 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Davis nasceu aproximadamente 1591, em Arley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, David Davis, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Angharrad Jevan, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Martha Wakeman em 30 de novembro de 1621, em Bewdley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 21 de outubro de 1659, em Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Davis родился около 1591 г. в местности арли, ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени David Davis было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Angharrad Jevan, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Martha Wakeman 30 ноября 1621 г. в местности бюдли, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер перед 21 октябрь 1659 г. в местности нюхавен тоун, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Davis nació aproximadamente 1591, en Arley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, David Davis, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Angharrad Jevan, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Martha Wakeman el 30 de noviembre de 1621, en Bewdley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió antes de 21 de octubre de 1659, en Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.
